mnemonics.title: Main Function
mnemonics.content:
M - Master entry point of the program
A - Arguments are passed through argc and argv
I - Initializes the program
N - Never forget to return an integer value

mnemonics.title: Header Files
mnemonics.content:
H - Helps to organize code
E - Enables code reuse
A - Avoids multiple definitions
D - Declares functions, variables, and classes
E - Enhances code readability
R - Reduces compilation time

acronyms.usage: Used to denote the standard library namespace

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a header file as a container that holds declarations of functions, variables, and classes.
Think of the main function as the entrance to a program, where the program starts execution.

analogies.analogy: A header file is like a phonebook that contains phone numbers (declarations) of people (functions, variables, and classes) you want to contact (use) in your program.
The main function is like the main door of a house, where you enter the house (program) and start exploring (executing) the different rooms (functions).
